title: cuphea odonellii
intro: Cuphea odonellii, also known as the Mexican heather or Mexican plume poppy, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It is a shrub-like perennial plant that belongs to the Lythraceae family, known for its small, delicate flowers and feathery foliage. Cuphea odonellii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system.
